description_long: This picnic table is constructed from several wide, flat wooden planks forming a rectangular tabletop, supported by robust wooden legs and crossbeams. Two long benches, also made of wood, run parallel to the longer sides of the table and are securely attached to the main frame. The wood has a natural, dark brown finish with visible grain and black fasteners at the joints, giving it a rustic and sturdy appearance. The table's support structure includes diagonal braces and horizontal beams, enhancing stability and durability. The design is practical and functional, suitable for group seating and outdoor use. There is no visible hole in the center of the tabletop for an umbrella.
description: A sturdy, dark brown wooden picnic table with attached benches and visible black fasteners, designed for group seating. The structure features robust legs, crossbeams, and diagonal braces for stability.
description_view_0: Front view showing the wide tabletop and one attached bench, with visible fasteners and sturdy legs.
description_view_1: Top-down view highlighting the plank arrangement of the tabletop and benches, as well as the crossbeam structure beneath.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, showing the opposite bench and the same robust construction.
description_view_3: Top-down view from the opposite side, again emphasizing the plank layout and support beams.
attribute_tabletop_support_structure: The tabletop is supported by four sturdy wooden legs connected by horizontal crossbeams and diagonal braces, providing stability and strength to the entire structure.
